title: Odyssey, Book 22: The Slaughter of the Suitors
description: Book 22 of Homer's Odyssey: The Slaughter of the Suitors.
introduction: Book 22 of the Odyssey: The Slaughter of the Suitors. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: Odysseus stripped off his rags and leaped to the threshold with his bow. He shot Antinous through the throat. The suitors reached for weapons but found none — Telemachus had removed them. Odysseus, Telemachus, Eumaeus, and the cowherd killed every suitor. The disloyal serving women were hanged. The hall was cleansed with fire and sulfur.
